title: Enhancement Ideas Log
organization: Two Marshalls Studios - Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Preserves verified and deferred enhancement ideas under repository-first governance without interrupting the current approved TMS-OS work path.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document is the permanent Enhancement Ideas Log for Two Marshalls Studios and the Studio Operating System.
It preserves future improvement ideas without allowing them to interrupt the current approved milestone, work-session objective, stabilization activity, documentation reconciliation, or bug-fix path.
The log provides a controlled location where enhancement ideas can be captured, classified, related to permanent records, reviewed later, and either approved, rejected, deferred, or closed.
An entry in this log records an idea. It does not authorize implementation.

sections.title: Repository-First Governance Boundary
paragraphs: ENH-LOG-001 is a permanent repository-controlled document.
The TMS-OS repository is the authoritative source for the approved enhancement log, related structured records, implementation history, and final status.
The Studio Portal presents the approved log but does not independently authorize, modify, or replace the repository record.

Chat history, screenshots, temporary notes, exported files, and external storage may provide evidence or recovery support, but enhancement ideas must be reconciled into this repository document before they are treated as permanently captured.

sections.title: Permanent Enhancement Rule
paragraphs: Enhancement ideas must be preserved from the beginning of the project forward.
A valuable idea must not be lost merely because it is outside the current scope.
A valuable idea must also not be implemented merely because it has been captured.
The approved current milestone, current work-session objective, active bug fix, and stabilization requirements take priority unless Stephen explicitly approves a scope change.
When an enhancement is not part of the current approved work, record it here and continue the approved path.

sections.title: Enhancement Versus Required Work
paragraphs: An enhancement adds, expands, improves, automates, redesigns, or accelerates capability beyond the currently approved requirement.
A bug fix restores approved behavior that is broken.
A documentation correction aligns a permanent record with verified repository facts.
A required completion item finishes work already approved in the roadmap, milestone, procedure, or current-state record.
A risk control may be required work when it is necessary to preserve safety, rollback readiness, source-of-truth integrity, or controlled execution.
When classification is uncertain, stop and obtain approval rather than silently treating an enhancement as required work.

sections.title: How Enhancement Ideas Are Handled
items: Capture the idea as soon as it appears.
Assign or confirm a unique enhancement ID.
Record the title, department, status, priority, summary, reason, source, and related records.
State whether the idea is historical, newly captured, approved, deferred, rejected, or built.
Do not build the idea during the current work session unless Stephen explicitly approves it.
Keep the current work-session objective focused.
Record dependencies, risks, technical debt, and required decisions when known.
Review the enhancement later during planning, roadmap review, or a dedicated approved enhancement session.
When implemented, preserve the implementation session, tests, files, decisions, and final result.

rows: The idea has been recorded but has not yet been evaluated.
The idea remains potentially valuable but is not part of the current approved work.
The idea is being evaluated for purpose, scope, timing, dependencies, risk, and value.
Stephen has approved the idea for a future milestone, roadmap item, or work session.
The approved idea has been sequenced into a defined future implementation path.
Implementation has begun within an approved work session.
The idea cannot proceed until a required dependency, decision, resource, or technical capability is available.
The idea was reviewed and intentionally declined.
The idea has been implemented, tested, documented, and closed.
The idea was replaced by a newer approved design, decision, or implementation.

sections.title: Enhancement Priority Definitions
paragraphs: Critical - reserved for an approved enhancement that becomes necessary to preserve core operation, data integrity, controlled execution, or recovery. A critical classification does not bypass approval.
High - offers substantial strategic, operational, governance, continuity, or efficiency value.
Medium - offers meaningful improvement but can wait until higher-priority approved work is complete.
Low - offers limited or convenience-focused improvement and may remain deferred indefinitely.
Priority reflects potential value and sequencing importance. It does not itself authorize implementation.

sections.title: Required Fields for Enhancement Records
items: Unique enhancement ID
Title
Department or functional area
Current status
Priority
Summary
Why the enhancement matters
Source or trigger
Date or work session captured when known
Related work sessions
Related roadmap items or milestones
Related decisions, standards, procedures, risks, bugs, lessons, or technical debt
Dependencies
Owner or review area when known
Next review action
Approval record when approved
Implementation result when built
Verification or test result when built

sections.title: Historical Baseline Reconciliation
paragraphs: The original recovered enhancement baseline contained four records.
ENH-2026-0001 has been reframed from Google Drive Publishing to repository-controlled documentation publishing and distribution so external locations remain secondary to the repository.
ENH-2026-0002 remains deferred but now explicitly requires controlled validation, approval, lifecycle, transaction, and repository-write boundaries.
ENH-2026-0003 remains deferred pending stable navigation and search foundations.

ENH-2026-0004 is now recorded as Built because the work-session closeout, documentation proposal, transaction, rollback, lifecycle, and state-control foundations were implemented across later approved work sessions.
Four additional verified enhancement ideas have been added to preserve current governance and continuity opportunities without expanding the active WS086 scope.

sections.title: Enhancement Review Procedure
paragraphs: 1. Select the enhancement for review.
2. Verify that the enhancement record is complete enough for evaluation.
3. Confirm whether the idea still solves a current verified need.
4. Review its relationship to the roadmap, architecture, current state, risks, technical debt, and active milestone.
5. Identify dependencies and whether required foundations already exist.
6. Determine the effect on source-of-truth integrity, controlled execution, testing, documentation, and recovery.
7. Assign or confirm status and priority.
8. Record Stephen's approval when the idea is approved.
9. Place approved work into the roadmap or a dedicated work-session plan.
10. Do not begin implementation until the approved session starts.
11. After implementation, record files, tests, decisions, risks, documentation changes, and final outcome.
12. Change the enhancement to Built only after implementation and verification are complete.

sections.title: Relationship to Current Work
paragraphs: This log does not authorize immediate implementation.
Its purpose is to protect the active work path by giving valuable ideas a permanent controlled location.

At the Work Session 086 checkpoint, the active path is permanent-document reconciliation, installation testing, governance alignment, and controlled completion of the verified repository documentation set.
New enhancements discovered during this work must be recorded and deferred unless Stephen explicitly changes the approved scope.

sections.title: Relationship to Other Permanent Records
paragraphs: ROADMAP-001 controls approved sequencing and future milestones.
STATE-001 records the current approved checkpoint and active work.
ARCH-001 controls architecture and source-of-truth boundaries.
DOC-STATE-001 records the state of permanent documentation.
DOC-INDEX-001 records the permanent-document inventory and relationships.
RISK-001 records risks that may affect an enhancement or result from implementing it.
TECH-DEBT-001 records verified deferred technical obligations, which are not automatically enhancements.
BUG-HIST-001 records defects and fixes, which must not be mislabeled as enhancements.
DEC-LOG-001 records approvals, rejections, and major design decisions.
LESSON-001 records verified lessons that may generate future enhancement ideas.
STD-001 and PROC-001 through PROC-004 define the standards and procedures governing capture, review, implementation, testing, and documentation.

sections.title: Work Session 086 Reconciliation State
paragraphs: ENH-LOG-001 has been reconciled to the repository-first Work Session 086 governance baseline.
The earlier Google documentation recovery wording has been replaced with repository authority and controlled external publishing language.
The enhancement statuses, priorities, required fields, review process, and relationships have been expanded.
The backlog now contains eight preserved enhancement records, including one verified built enhancement and seven deferred enhancements.
This content is repository reconciled but requires installation and operational testing before final verification.

sections.title: Installation Verification Requirements
items: Confirm ENH-LOG-001 loads from the approved repository path.
Confirm the Documentation Catalog displays the document.
Confirm Shared Search finds the document by ID, title, backlog terms, and repository-first terms.
Confirm the Shared Document Viewer renders all sections and the enhancement backlog table.
Confirm related documents and revision history display correctly.
Confirm the browser console reports no new JSON, search, viewer, or rendering errors.
Do not change the document to fully verified until all required tests pass.

sections.title: Next Controlled Action
paragraphs: Install this complete replacement file at pages/documents/ENH-LOG-001.json.
Perform the required catalog, search, viewer, relationship, revision-history, and browser-console tests.
Do not commit or push until the installation test passes and the documentation reconciliation checkpoint is approved.
After successful testing, verify whether the 20-document permanent repository set is fully reconciled and prepare the appropriate controlled session checkpoint.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: Two Marshalls Studios will preserve enhancement ideas from the beginning of the project forward.
Ideas will be captured, organized, reviewed, approved, rejected, deferred, implemented, and closed through a controlled repository-first process.
No enhancement record overrides the approved roadmap, current milestone, work-session objective, bug-fix requirement, risk control, or human approval boundary.
ENH-LOG-001 is the permanent repository-controlled enhancement record for TMS-OS.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

Fully reconciled the Enhancement Ideas Log to repository-first governance, expanded enhancement controls and review states, reconciled the historical backlog, recorded the automated closeout enhancement as built, and added verified deferred enhancement opportunities.

verification.method: Reconciled against the verified TMS-OS repository architecture, permanent documentation set, roadmap, current-state records, implemented session-automation history, and the approved Work Session 086 repository-first governance baseline.
